73745.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Standard Chartered compares Ethereum to Amazon during 2001 dot-com bubble burst
73745.body:
Trump vows to codify 'future-proof' digital asset market structure | Samsung units to acquire $408 million stake in Upbit operator Dunamu | US charges Google engineer with using internal search data to pocket $1.2 million on Polymarket | JPMorgan says bitcoin and gold ETF outflows point to 'cooling' debasement trade amid hopes for Iran-US deal |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Thursday! *Bitcoin* briefly slipped ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402816/bitcoin-falls-below-73000?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) below $73,000 as *BlackRock's IBIT* recorded its second-largest daily outflow since launch, with the broader crypto pullback driven by ETF redemptions, profit-taking, and macro uncertainty, according to analysts.

In today's newsletter, *Standard Chartered* compares *Ethereum* to *Amazon* during the 2001 dot-com bubble burst, *President Trump* says he will lead efforts to codify a "future-proof" crypto market structure, *Samsung* affiliates are set to purchase a 4% stake in *Dunamu* , and more.

Meanwhile, the *Sui* blockchain experienced ( https://www.theblock.co/post/402907/sui-blockchain-experiences-second-stall-in-five-months?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) a second "stall" in five months as developers work to implement a fix.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*📉 Standard Chartered compares Ethereum to Amazon during 2001 dot-com bubble burst, says ETH will catch up to internal metrics*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/402864/standard-chartered-ethereum-amazon-dot-com-bubble-eth-will-catch-up?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Standard Chartered* said *ether's* recent price weakness does not reflect *Ethereum's* improving internal metrics, comparing *ETH's* setup to *Amazon* during the 2001 dot-com crash.

* Global Head of Digital Assets Research *Geoffrey Kendrick* said Ethereum transaction numbers and total value locked measured in ETH terms remain near all-time highs despite ether falling roughly 57% from its August 2025 peak.
* Standard Chartered maintained its long-term ether targets of $4,000 by the end of 2026 and $40,000 by the end of 2030, arguing that ETH will eventually c

73744.subject:
Crypto Biz: Crypto infrastructure spending rises as ETF appetite
 cools
73744.body:
Crypto Biz Newsletter

Автор: Cointelegraph


Автор: Cointelegraph

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/crypto-biz-infrastructure-spending-rises-etf-appetite-cools-frecc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BcJLj7wA5QgyHqjlgc4zxFQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=09yOcTq_j1Hcg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpprzhm0~zb-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpprzhm0-zb
  
Institutional crypto activity took a mixed turn this week as major financial institutions continued expanding into digital assets even while investor demand for Bitcoin funds weakened.  Mastercard secured regulatory approval to grow its crypto operations in New York, underscoring how traditional payment companies are continuing to build around stablecoins, tokenized payments and blockchain infrastructure. At the same time, Ether treasury companies kept accumulating ETH and increasing staking activity as they searched for ways to stand apart from passive crypto investment products. But signs of slowing investor appetite also emerged. US spot Bitcoin ETFs extended their outflow streak, pushing cumulative 2026 inflows closer to negative territory after another week of institutional withdrawals. Mastercard finally cracks New York’s crypto code Mastercard has secured a coveted BitLicense from the New York Department of Financial Services (NYDFS), giving the payments giant regulatory clearance to expand its crypto and stablecoin operations in one of the toughest jurisdictions in the United States.  The timing is notable, given that Mastercard is aggressively building crypto partnerships across stablecoins, tokenized payments and onchain commerce. The company already works with several blockchain networks and wallet providers, including MetaMask, as part of its broader strategy to make digital assets interoperable with existing card infrastructure. New York’s BitLicense regime has historically been an obstacle for crypto companies because of its stringent compliance requirements, with many companies avoiding the state altogether rather than endure the licensing process. Mastercard’s approval signals that regulators are increasingly willing to accommodate established financial institutions entering the digital asset sector, especially those focused on payments and settlement rails instead of speculative trading. Source: Mastercard Continue Reading… Bitmine doubles down on Ether as Tom Lee calls for a “supercycle” BitMine Immersion Technologies purchased 111,942 Ether last week, its largest ETH acquisition of 2026 so far, after the cryptocurrency briefly fell below $2,200. The buying spree comes as company chairman Tom Lee renewed his bullish thesis that Ethereum is entering a long-term crypto “supercycle” fueled by tokenization and AI-

OpenAI is still working with Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley on its IPO filing.¹

But according to Reuters, some major U.S. index and mutual funds are already preparing for what could be one of the largest tech IPOs in years.²

OpenAI’s recent momentum helps explain why:

- Defeated Elon Musk in a major courtroom victory.³
- Beat Anthropic’s Q1 revenue by about $1 billion.⁴
- Record $122B round joined by Amazon, NVIDIA, and SoftBank.⁵

A confidential filing does not guarantee that an IPO will occur. These reports don't imply specific results for investors with exposure to OpenAI on StartEngine Private. Past performance is not indicative of future results. There's no guarantee that revenue, funding, or valuation will continue to grow, or that OpenAI will be profitable. Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-may-28-oleh-bezuhlyi-hfhvf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BjUcXAyaSSeut4FAQ9aQObQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0al9tnVUa5Hcg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpppu6g1~iu-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpppu6g1-iu
  
🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Institutional caution dominates as record $733M ETF outflows and geopolitical risks pressure Bitcoin toward $73,000. Despite the dip, landmark regulatory progress and product expansions like the first spot BNB ETF signal long-term structural maturation. 🚨BlackRock’s IBIT Sees Record $528M Outflow as Bitcoin Nears $73K Bitcoin price fell below critical support levels, triggering a massive $733 million net outflow from U.S. spot ETFs on Wednesday. BlackRock’s IBIT recorded its second-largest daily outflow in history, signaling a sharp reversal in institutional sentiment amid macroeconomic uncertainty. Read full story 🏛️Trump Pledges "Future-Proof" Crypto Market Structure in Major Regulatory Push President Donald Trump vowed to codify a permanent and "future-proof" regulatory framework for digital assets to prevent future administrative crackdowns. The announcement coincides with the White House review of prediction market rules, marking a coordinated effort to cement the U.S. as a global crypto hub. See details 💰DTCC Partners with Stellar to Tokenize Real-World Assets on Public Blockchain The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC) and Stellar Development Foundation announced plans to tokenize DTC-custodied assets on the Stellar network. This landmark collaboration aims to bridge traditional securities with digital ecosystems, targeting highly liquid assets like U.S. Treasury bills and index ETFs. Read full story 📈VanEck Debuts First U.S. Spot BNB ETF Under Ticker VBNB VanEck has officially launched the VanEck BNB ETF (VBNB) on Nasdaq, becoming the first asset manager to offer direct spot exposure to the native token of the BNB Chain. The physically-backed fund carries a 0.39% management fee and utilizes Anchorage Digital Bank for secure, federally-chartered custody. See details 📊Standard Chartered: Ethereum is the "Amazon of Blockchains" Following Market Shakeout Standard Chartered analysts have likened Ethereum’s current market cycle to Amazon’s recovery following the 2001 dot-com bubble burst. The bank reaffirmed its long-term ETH price target of $4,000 by year-end, citing dominance in the $2 trillion stablecoin and tokenized RWA sectors. Read full story ⚖️Federal GENIUS Act Officially Takes Effect, Standardizing U.S. Stablecoin Markets The Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S.

our Charlemagne column

73728.subject: Token Markets Grow Up
73728.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

GM and happy Thursday!

Crypto struggles while equities hang out at highs. Miners get the best of both worlds as IREN rallies off of a new Blackwell purchase agreement.

Below, we break down the launch of the Transparency Alliance, Blockworks' effort to bring standardized token disclosures to a historically opaque industry.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

Strength in equities is not matched in crypto. As the S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures traded a new all-time high yesterday, BTC traded down to $74,025, its lowest level over the past 38 days. With this weakness, the majority of crypto indices are red on the week, with the Privacy sector as the top loser (-12.7%). 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/c0e46c74-b4e4-427d-9f0c-405ce3582f76/image.png?t=1779971399)
Caption: 

Focusing on the winners, Crypto Miners are at the top of the leaderboard, up 18.4% on the week. IREN jumped 14% yesterday following the announcement of a $1.6B purchase agreement for air-cooled Blackwell systems supplied by Dell. This equipment could increase IREN’s annual revenue by 19%. The announcement renewed momentum across the Crypto Miners sector, with most names trading at or near recent highs, and reminding us where the hot ball of money is.  

_— __[Luke](https://x.com/0xMether)_

———————————————————————————

# Formalizing Disclosure in Token Markets

Token markets have an unsolved information asymmetry problem.

When you buy a stock, you get a prospectus, a 10-K, and quarterly filings with audited numbers, disclosed insiders, and legally binding representations about material information. The disclosure burden may be imperfect, but the framework is legible. When purchasing a token, the burden of collecting this information from various governance forums, Discord channels, X/Twitter accounts, and blog posts falls on the holder. Market maker agreements, if they exist, are buried or undisclosed. This gap between what issuers know and what tokenholders can access is structural, and it has been the engine behind most of crypto's worst moments of the last decade.

Today, Blockworks launches the [Transparency Alliance](https://blockworks.com/insights/blockworks-launches-transparency-alliance) to address this directly.

The Alliance formalizes the [Token Transparency Framework](https://blockworks.com/token-transparency) (TTF), which Blockworks first introduced in June 2025, into an industry-wide standard backed by t
